c2023-a3-tsuchinshanatlas.png.title: Comet C/2023 A3 (Tsuchinshan–ATLAS)
c2023-a3-tsuchinshanatlas.png.description: Comet C/2023 A3 (Tsuchinshan–ATLAS) was discovered in 2023 and reached naked-eye visibility in 2024. It is a long-period comet that passed through the inner solar system.

horsehead-flame-nebula.png.title: Horsehead and Flame Nebula [B33 / NGC 2024]
horsehead-flame-nebula.png.description: The Horsehead Nebula is a dark nebula in Orion, silhouetted against the emission nebula IC 434. The Flame Nebula (NGC 2024) is an emission nebula nearby, both part of the Orion molecular cloud complex.

m101_1.jpg.title: Pinwheel Galaxy [M101]
m101_1.jpg.description: The Pinwheel Galaxy is a face-on spiral in Ursa Major with asymmetric arms and vigorous star formation. It is one of the largest and most detailed spirals visible in amateur telescopes.

m101_2.jpg.title: Pinwheel Galaxy [M101]
m101_2.jpg.description: The Pinwheel Galaxy is a face-on spiral in Ursa Major with asymmetric arms and vigorous star formation. It is one of the largest and most detailed spirals visible in amateur telescopes.

m104.png.title: Sombrero Galaxy [M104]
m104.png.description: The Sombrero Galaxy is an edge-on spiral in Virgo with a bright bulge and a prominent dust lane. Its distinctive shape makes it one of the most photographed galaxies.

m106.png.title: M106 [NGC 4258]
m106.png.description: M106 is a spiral galaxy in Canes Venatici with anomalous arms that suggest a supermassive black hole activity. It is one of the largest and brightest nearby galaxies.

m27.png.title: Dumbbell Nebula [M27]
m27.png.description: The Dumbbell Nebula is a large planetary nebula in Vulpecula and one of the brightest in the sky. Its elongated shape gives it the name and makes it a popular target for imaging.

m31_1.jpg.title: Andromeda Galaxy [M31]
m31_1.jpg.description: The Andromeda Galaxy is the nearest large spiral galaxy to the Milky Way, about 2.5 million light-years away. It is on a collision course with our galaxy and will merge with it in several billion years.

m31_2.png.title: Andromeda Galaxy [M31]
m31_2.png.description: The Andromeda Galaxy is the nearest large spiral galaxy to the Milky Way, about 2.5 million light-years away. It is on a collision course with our galaxy and will merge with it in several billion years.

m33-triangulum-galaxy.jpg.title: Triangulum Galaxy [M33]
m33-triangulum-galaxy.jpg.description: M33 is a face-on spiral galaxy in Triangulum and the third-largest member of our Local Group. It lacks a prominent central bulge and is a popular target for astrophotography.

m45.jpg.title: Pleiades [M45]
m45.jpg.description: The Pleiades is an open star cluster in Taurus, famous for its blue reflection nebulosity. It is among the nearest star clusters and has been known since antiquity.

m45_2.jpg.title: Pleiades [M45]
m45_2.jpg.description: The Pleiades is an open star cluster in Taurus, famous for its blue reflection nebulosity. It is among the nearest star clusters and has been known since antiquity.

m57-the-ring-nebula.jpg.title: Ring Nebula [M57]
m57-the-ring-nebula.jpg.description: The Ring Nebula is a planetary nebula in Lyra, the glowing shell of a dying sun-like star. It is one of the best-known planetary nebulae and shows a distinct ring shape in small telescopes.

m81-bodes-galaxy-1.jpg.title: Bode's Galaxy and Cigar Galaxy [M81 & M82]
m81-bodes-galaxy-1.jpg.description: Bode's Galaxy (M81) is a grand-design spiral in Ursa Major and one of the brightest galaxies in the night sky. The Cigar Galaxy (M82) is the starburst companion often imaged with it; together they form a striking pair.

m81-bodes-galaxy-2.png.title: Bode's Galaxy [M81]
m81-bodes-galaxy-2.png.description: Bode's Galaxy is a grand-design spiral in Ursa Major and one of the brightest galaxies in the night sky. It forms a striking pair with the irregular galaxy M82.

m81-bodes-galaxy.png.title: Bode's Galaxy [M81]
m81-bodes-galaxy.png.description: Bode's Galaxy is a grand-design spiral in Ursa Major and one of the brightest galaxies in the night sky. It forms a striking pair with the irregular galaxy M82.

m82-the-cigar-galaxy.png.title: Cigar Galaxy [M82]
m82-the-cigar-galaxy.png.description: The Cigar Galaxy is a starburst galaxy in Ursa Major, undergoing intense star formation. It is often imaged together with M81 and shows prominent dust lanes.

mars-jupiter-saturn.jpg.title: Mars, Jupiter and Saturn
mars-jupiter-saturn.jpg.description: A planetary conjunction or grouping of Mars, Jupiter, and Saturn in the same frame. Such alignments occur when the planets appear close together in the sky from our viewpoint on Earth.

moon_crater_1.jpg.title: Moon — crater detail
moon_crater_1.jpg.description: Close-up of a lunar crater showing the rugged terrain of the Moon's surface. Craters are formed by asteroid and comet impacts over billions of years.

moon_crater_2.jpg.title: Moon — crater detail
moon_crater_2.jpg.description: Close-up of a lunar crater showing the rugged terrain of the Moon's surface. Craters are formed by asteroid and comet impacts over billions of years.

moon_crater_3.jpg.title: Moon — crater detail
moon_crater_3.jpg.description: Close-up of a lunar crater showing the rugged terrain of the Moon's surface. Craters are formed by asteroid and comet impacts over billions of years.

moon_crater_4.jpg.title: Moon — crater detail
moon_crater_4.jpg.description: Close-up of a lunar crater showing the rugged terrain of the Moon's surface. Craters are formed by asteroid and comet impacts over billions of years.

moon_crater_5.jpg.title: Moon — crater detail
moon_crater_5.jpg.description: Close-up of a lunar crater showing the rugged terrain of the Moon's surface. Craters are formed by asteroid and comet impacts over billions of years.

moon_crater_6.jpg.title: Moon — crater detail
moon_crater_6.jpg.description: Close-up of a lunar crater showing the rugged terrain of the Moon's surface. Craters are formed by asteroid and comet impacts over billions of years.

ngc-7380-the-wizard-nebula.jpg.title: Wizard Nebula [NGC 7380]
ngc-7380-the-wizard-nebula.jpg.description: The Wizard Nebula is an open cluster embedded in an emission nebula in Cepheus. The gas and dust form shapes that suggest a wizard or sorcerer in long-exposure images.

orion_1.jpg.title: Orion Nebula [M42]
orion_1.jpg.description: The Orion Nebula is a diffuse nebula in Orion and one of the brightest nebulae in the sky, visible to the naked eye. It is a stellar nursery where new stars are forming and is about 1,344 light-years away.

orion_2.jpg.title: Orion Nebula [M42]
orion_2.jpg.description: The Orion Nebula is a diffuse nebula in Orion and one of the brightest nebulae in the sky, visible to the naked eye. It is a stellar nursery where new stars are forming and is about 1,344 light-years away.

rosette-nebula.png.title: Rosette Nebula [NGC 2237]
rosette-nebula.png.description: The Rosette Nebula is a large circular emission nebula in Monoceros, with an open cluster at its center. Its flower-like shape makes it a favorite target for astrophotographers.

western-veil-nebula.jpg.title: Western Veil Nebula [NGC 6960]
western-veil-nebula.jpg.description: The Veil Nebula is a large supernova remnant in Cygnus, the expanding shell of a star that exploded thousands of years ago. The western section appears as delicate filaments in narrowband or processed images.
